technician_notes: PM visit on RTU-48XL at rooftop level 3. Unit running on arrival, cooling mode. Pulled and inspected both filters - 2-inch pleated filters loaded with debris, replaced. Condenser coil had moderate dirt accumulation on inlet face, coil cleaned with low-pressure rinse. Checked refrigerant pressures: suction 68 PSI, discharge 215 PSI, within spec for R-410A at ambient 72F. Amp draw on compressor: 18.2A on L1, 17.9A on L2, nameplate max 21A. Contactor contacts showed minor pitting, not yet at replacement threshold but noted. Drain pan clean, no standing water. Blower belt tension checked and adjusted. Economizer dampers cycled manually, actuator responding normally.
anomalies_flagged: Contactor contacts showing early pitting - monitor at next PM
resolution: Filters replaced, condenser coil cleaned, blower belt tensioned. Unit returned to service in normal operating condition.
compliance_notes: Lockout/tagout applied to unit disconnect before filter access and coil cleaning. Electrical compartment opened with appropriate PPE. Refrigerant not vented - system remained sealed throughout.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Emergency call - no heat. Ambient 34F outside. Arrived to find unit locked out on high pressure fault. Checked outdoor unit: condenser fan not running. Tested capacitor - reading 6.2 uF on a 7.5 uF rated cap, 17% low, outside tolerance. Replaced capacitor, fan started immediately. Cleared fault, unit initiated defrost cycle. Monitored through defrost, exited normally at 8 min. Suction pressure 110 PSI, discharge 285 PSI in heat pump mode at 34F ambient - acceptable. Indoor unit blower running, supply temp 95F at register. Amp draw on compressor 14.1A, within nameplate range.
anomalies_flagged: Condenser fan capacitor failed - out of tolerance by 17%
resolution: Failed condenser fan run capacitor replaced. Fault cleared, unit restored to heating operation. Defrost cycle verified normal.
compliance_notes: Lockout/tagout on outdoor disconnect before capacitor replacement. Capacitor discharged with insulated resistor before handling. Refrigerant system not opened.

technician_notes: Annual safety inspection on SL280V. Checked heat exchanger with mirror and light - no visible cracks or corrosion in primary or secondary exchanger. Gas pressure at manifold 3.48 in WC, within spec of 3.2-3.8. Measured CO at flue: 12 ppm, acceptable. Ignitor resistance 52 ohms, within range. Flame sensor cleaned with fine steel wool, microamp reading before cleaning was 0.9 uA (low), after cleaning 3.4 uA - good. Blower motor amp draw 6.8A on high speed. Checked all safeties: limit switch, pressure switch, rollout switch - all cycling correctly. Filter was 4 months old, still within service life.
anomalies_flagged: Flame sensor fouled - microamp reading at 0.9 uA before cleaning, borderline
resolution: Flame sensor cleaned. All safeties verified. Heat exchanger integrity confirmed. Unit operating normally.
compliance_notes: Gas supply shut off at manual valve before any internal access. Combustion analysis performed with calibrated analyzer. CO levels within acceptable threshold per ANSI Z21.47.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: Call received: unit not cooling, thermostat showing call for cool but no response. On arrival, outdoor unit compressor humming but not starting, tripping internal overload every 90 seconds. Measured compressor winding resistance: common-to-run 3.2 ohms, common-to-start 4.8 ohms, run-to-start 8.0 ohms - within spec. Checked start capacitor: 45 uF rated, reading 41.2 uF, acceptable. Checked line voltage at compressor: L1-L2 reading 192V, should be 208-240V. Traced back to panel - one leg weak at 96V. Called building maintenance, confirmed loose lug at main panel. Building electrician tightened connection, voltage restored to 208V. Compressor started normally on next call.
anomalies_flagged: Low line voltage on L1 - 96V on one leg due to loose panel lug (building electrical issue)
resolution: Compressor not at fault. Building electrician corrected loose main panel lug. Voltage restored to 208V. Unit started and verified cooling normally. Refrigerant pressures normal: suction 120 PSI, discharge 285 PSI.
compliance_notes: Did not open electrical panel - referred to licensed building electrician per NEC requirements. Lockout applied to unit disconnect during voltage testing. PPE worn throughout.

technician_notes: End-of-season PM. Ambient 91F. Inspected and replaced 1-inch filter - heavily loaded. Outdoor condenser coil cleaned top-down, significant cottonwood debris on inlet side. Refrigerant check: suction 75 PSI, discharge 230 PSI - slightly low suction for ambient conditions, subcooling 10F (should be 8-12F, acceptable). Measured superheat at 12F. Contactor checked - contacts good, coil draws 0.38A. Capacitor: dual-run 45+5 uF, reading 44.1/4.8 uF, within tolerance. Condensate drain flushed with diluted bleach solution - had algae growth at exit point. Checked drain pan: no standing water but slime line at pan edges cleaned.
anomalies_flagged: Algae growth in condensate drain - flushed and treated
resolution: Filter replaced, coil cleaned, condensate drain flushed. System operating within normal parameters for season-end.
compliance_notes: Unit de-energized at disconnect for filter and coil service. Condensate treatment with bleach solution - diluted per manufacturer guidance, no chemical disposal concerns.

technician_notes: No heat call during business hours, occupants reporting 58F in building. Unit found running but in fault. Fault code retrieved: E4 - low pressure lockout. Outdoor ambient 28F. Checked refrigerant: suction side 42 PSI on R-410A, way too low for heating mode. Checked for obvious leaks at service valves, coil connections - no oily residue visible but suction line frost at expansion valve. Leak search with electronic detector - found leak at Schrader valve core on suction service port, valve was partially open and leaking. Tightened and capped. Added 1.5 lbs R-410A to bring suction to 112 PSI in heat pump mode. Cleared fault, unit entered heating. Monitored 15 minutes - holding pressure.
anomalies_flagged: Refrigerant leak at suction Schrader valve core - partial valve failure
System was low 1.5 lbs R-410A
resolution: Schrader valve core replaced at suction service port. 1.5 lbs R-410A added. System pressures restored to spec. Fault cleared, heating confirmed.
compliance_notes: R-410A handled per EPA Section 608. Technician holds 608 Universal certification. Recovery equipment staged but not needed as only charging performed. Refrigerant log updated. PPE including safety glasses worn during refrigerant work.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Pre-season cooling inspection. System not run since October. Started unit in cooling mode - compressor started cleanly, no hard start. Suction 70 PSI, discharge 220 PSI at 68F ambient, looks good. Superheat 14F. Subcooling 11F. Indoor coil: found significant dust on coil face, cleaned with soft brush - coil not clogged but needed attention. Checked blower wheel - minor debris on leading edges, brushed clean. Measured static pressure: 0.82 in WC total external, slightly elevated, checked duct at main trunk - register in rear bedroom partially blocked by furniture. Notified homeowner. Checked all electrical connections in both units, torqued terminals.
anomalies_flagged: Elevated static pressure - traced to blocked register (homeowner issue)
Indoor coil dust accumulation
resolution: Indoor coil and blower wheel cleaned. Homeowner advised to move furniture blocking rear bedroom register. System operating within spec otherwise.
compliance_notes: Lockout/tagout on both indoor air handler and outdoor unit disconnects before accessing electrical compartments. Refrigerant pressures checked, no system access required.

technician_notes: Call: furnace running but no heat coming from registers. Arrived to find inducer running, ignitor glowing, but no ignition. Gas valve clicking. Checked gas supply: shutoff open, pressure at valve inlet 7.2 in WC. Operated valve manually - no gas flow audible. Tested valve coil: 24V at terminals confirmed. Measured coil resistance: 37 ohms on primary, open circuit on secondary winding. Gas valve secondary coil failed. Shut off gas, replaced valve. Re-lit, monitored first 3 cycles - ignition consistent, flame stable, CO at flue 8 ppm. Static pressure switch verified opening on inducer.
anomalies_flagged: Gas valve secondary coil open circuit - valve failed
resolution: Faulty gas valve replaced. Ignition verified on 3 consecutive cycles. Combustion analysis confirmed safe operation.
compliance_notes: Gas supply shut off at manual valve and verified with manometer before valve removal. Work area ventilated. Leak check performed at all gas connections post-installation using listed leak detection solution - no bubbles. Combustion analysis completed per best practices.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: Spring PM. Variable speed system - checked inverter drive board for error codes, none logged. Outdoor coil fins: bent fins on lower left quadrant, straightened with fin comb - approx 6 inch section. Refrigerant check in cooling mode at 65F ambient: suction 58 PSI, discharge 185 PSI. Superheat 8F. Subcooling 9F. All within acceptable range for variable speed at part load. Checked communicating thermostat - all zones reporting, no faults. Tested defrost board manually - initiated and terminated normally. Indoor air handler: filter replaced (was 5 months old), blower compartment clean.
anomalies_flagged: Bent condenser coil fins - minor physical damage, corrected
resolution: Fin comb applied to damaged coil section. Filter replaced. System operating normally.
compliance_notes: Lockout applied to both units before coil fin repair. Variable speed drive capacitors can retain charge - waited 5 minutes after de-energizing before opening inverter compartment per manufacturer service bulletin.

technician_notes: No cooling call, homeowner says unit runs but house not cooling. Ambient 98F. Unit found running continuously. Suction pressure 45 PSI on R-22 system - significantly low. Subcooling 2F (should be 8-12F), superheat 28F (elevated). Classic low refrigerant symptoms. Performed leak search: electronic detector hit positive at evaporator coil access panel area. Pulled access, found pinhole in evaporator coil - likely formicary corrosion. Verified with UV dye already in system (prior tech added). Coil replacement required but not stocked on truck. Added refrigerant temporarily to restore cooling - added 1.2 lbs R-22. Informed homeowner: coil needs replacement, R-22 cost and availability issues, recommend system upgrade discussion.
anomalies_flagged: Evaporator coil leak - formicary corrosion pinhole confirmed
System low on R-22 refrigerant
resolution: Temporary refrigerant charge added to restore cooling. Homeowner informed of coil failure and options. Coil replacement or system replacement recommended.
compliance_notes: R-22 is a regulated refrigerant. EPA Section 608 compliance maintained - no intentional venting. Technician holds 608 Universal cert. Refrigerant log updated with pounds added and system ID. Homeowner advised of R-22 phase-out and cost implications.

technician_notes: Fall PM transitioning unit to heating season. Switched unit controls to heating mode. Verified economizer lockout at ambient below 55F - dampers staying closed, correct. Gas heat section: checked burner assembly - slight scale buildup on burners, brushed clean. Heat exchanger inspection: no visible cracks, checked with CO detector at supply registers - 0 ppm above background. Manifold gas pressure 3.5 in WC. Induced draft motor amp draw 1.8A, normal. High limit switch tested - tripped at 195F, reset at 160F, within spec. Refrigerant section: service valves capped for winter. All electrical checked, no loose terminals found.
resolution: Unit transitioned to heating mode. Burners cleaned. All safeties verified. System ready for heating season.
compliance_notes: Lockout/tagout on unit main disconnect before accessing heat section. Gas supply shut off at unit valve before burner cleaning. Combustion verified post-service.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Trip hazard/leak call - water dripping from ceiling in hallway below air handler. Air handler in attic. Found condensate drain pan overflowing - primary drain completely blocked with algae mat. Secondary drain also partially blocked. Standing water in pan at 2 inches. Used wet vac to extract water from pan. Cleared primary drain with nitrogen flush - dislodged solid algae plug approximately 4 inches long. Flushed secondary drain. Poured diluted bleach treatment. Inspected ceiling below - minor water staining but no structural damage yet. Installed float switch on secondary drain pan as unit did not have one.
anomalies_flagged: Primary condensate drain fully blocked - algae mat
Secondary drain partially blocked
No float switch installed on drain pan - safety gap
resolution: Both drains cleared. Bleach treatment applied. Float switch added to secondary pan for future protection. Homeowner advised on quarterly drain treatment.
compliance_notes: Attic access - appropriate footwear and lighting used. Bleach solution handled per SDS - ventilated space. Electrical to float switch run from air handler control board per wiring diagram.

technician_notes: Inspection requested after homeowner smelled gas briefly last week. Could not reproduce gas odor on arrival. Checked all gas connections with soap solution: manifold, valve inlet, burner orifices, pressure taps - no bubbles. Gas pressure at meter 7.0 in WC, at manifold 3.5 in WC. Combustion analysis: CO 6 ppm at flue, O2 8.4%, CO2 8.1%. Checked heat exchanger carefully with camera scope through burner ports - found hairline crack in cell 3. CO at supply register measured 2 ppm above background during operation. Shut down unit immediately and red-tagged. Advised homeowner of safety hazard, gas shutoff recommended until repair.
anomalies_flagged: Hairline crack in heat exchanger cell 3 - confirmed with scope camera
CO detected at supply registers during operation - 2 ppm above background
resolution: Unit shut down and red-tagged. Gas supply closed at unit valve. Homeowner informed of carbon monoxide risk. Heat exchanger replacement or full unit replacement required.
compliance_notes: Unit de-energized and locked out after red-tag applied. Gas supply closed at unit manual valve. Homeowner advised to ventilate home and not restart unit. Incident documented per company safety protocol. CO detector placement reviewed with homeowner.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: Summer PM at 87F ambient. Outdoor coil: clean, no debris. Checked inverter for fault history - two faults logged in past 90 days, both DC bus undervoltage, likely related to power outage event per homeowner. Cleared fault log. Checked capacitors on inverter board - within spec visually, no bulging. Refrigerant in cooling: suction 130 PSI, discharge 310 PSI, subcooling 10F, superheat 9F. All good. Indoor filter replaced - 4 months. Checked all wire connections at both units. Blower amp draw 4.2A on medium speed, 6.8A on high. Drain clear, no standing water.
anomalies_flagged: Two historic DC bus undervoltage faults - attributed to power outage events, no current fault
resolution: Fault history cleared. Filter replaced. System operating normally. Homeowner advised fault codes were likely power-event related.
compliance_notes: Inverter drive board: waited 5 minutes after power removal before accessing per high-voltage capacitor discharge requirement. Lockout on both units applied.

technician_notes: End of cooling season inspection. Checked refrigerant charge: suction 70 PSI, discharge 225 PSI at 88F ambient. Subcooling 9F. Within normal range. Ran amp draw check: compressor 13.8A (nameplate max 17A), condenser fan 1.1A. Capacitor dual-run: 45+5 uF rated, reading 44.8/4.9 uF - good. Contactor coil: 24V confirmed, contacts look new. Outdoor coil: coated with fine red clay dust common in area, washed down. Condensate drain - dry season, drain trap dried out. Added water to trap to reseal. Noted one condenser fan blade had minor chip on tip - less than 5mm, not affecting balance, documented.
anomalies_flagged: Condenser fan blade tip chip - minor, documented for monitoring
resolution: Coil cleaned, drain trap recharged. System within spec. Fan blade chip documented, below replacement threshold.
compliance_notes: Lockout on unit disconnect for coil washing and amp check. Refrigerant accessed only for pressure readings, no system opened.

technician_notes: Unit tripped breaker overnight, building cold. Reset breaker, unit started then breaker tripped again at 45 seconds. Measured compressor amp draw at startup: 87A inrush (LRA spec is 95A), but holding amps climbing past 32A (RLA is 22A). Compressor drawing excessive running amps. Checked compressor windings: within spec. Checked refrigerant - suction pressure 30 PSI in heating mode (way low), discharge 490 PSI (very high) - compressor working against very high head pressure. Found head pressure relief valve weeping. Outdoor coil heavily iced over - defrost board failure, unit never defrosting. Replaced defrost control board, manual defrost initiated, ran 18 minutes to clear. Pressures normalized: suction 105 PSI, discharge 290 PSI.
anomalies_flagged: Defrost board failure - unit not defrosting, coil fully iced
Compressor overcurrent due to high head pressure from ice blockage
resolution: Defrost control board replaced. Manual defrost completed. Coil cleared, pressures normalized. Compressor running within amp spec after defrost.
compliance_notes: Lockout on main disconnect before board replacement. Compressor allowed to cool before restarting after overcurrent event. Refrigerant system not opened.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Spring PM ahead of cooling season. Replaced 16x25x1 filter - was 3 months old but heavily loaded due to recent construction in home. Outdoor unit: cleaned coil, found a wasp nest inside cabinet near contactor, removed carefully. Wasp nest residue cleaned from wiring, all insulation intact. Verified contactor - contacts acceptable, measured 24V coil pull-in. Refrigerant at 74F ambient: suction 72 PSI, discharge 228 PSI. Subcooling 10F, superheat 13F. Capacitor: 45+7.5 uF dual run, reading 44.2/7.1 uF - within spec. Indoor coil and blower clean. Drain clear.
anomalies_flagged: Wasp nest found in outdoor unit cabinet near contactor - removed
resolution: Filter replaced, coil cleaned, wasp nest removed and area cleaned. All electrical connections inspected, no damage from nest. System operating normally.
compliance_notes: PPE worn during wasp nest removal - face shield and gloves. Lockout applied before opening outdoor cabinet. Electrical insulation verified intact after nest removal.

technician_notes: Furnace running constantly but not reaching setpoint. Complaint: thermostat set to 72, house staying at 64. Checked supply and return temps: supply 118F, return 68F, delta T of 50F - below expected 55-70F range for this unit. Checked static pressure: 1.4 in WC total - very high. Pulled blower compartment, filter completely impacted - homeowner had not changed in 11 months. Replaced filter, rechecked static: 0.55 in WC, normal. Rechecked supply temp: 135F, delta T now 67F. Confirmed airflow restriction was causing limit switch cycling which reduced heat output. Checked heat exchanger incidentally - no cracks visible.
anomalies_flagged: Severely restricted filter causing high static pressure and limit switch cycling
resolution: Impacted filter replaced. Static pressure normalized. Unit now reaching setpoint within 20 minutes. Homeowner educated on filter replacement frequency.
compliance_notes: Lockout on furnace before blower compartment access. Gas supply remained on - no combustion work performed. Standard PPE.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: New install inspection at 30 days post-installation. Verified refrigerant charge per manufacturer charging chart at 58F ambient heating mode: suction 115 PSI, discharge 280 PSI. Subcooling 9F. Within spec. Checked all flare connections for tightness, no oily residue found. Verified airflow: measured 980 CFM with anemometer at supply registers vs. design 1000 CFM, within 5% tolerance. Communicating thermostat setup reviewed - all sensors reporting correctly. Checked line set insulation: one section of suction line insulation had gap of 8 inches at elbow, corrected with foam insulation and tape. Static pressure 0.68 in WC - acceptable for VS system.
anomalies_flagged: Suction line insulation gap at elbow - 8 inches, corrected
resolution: Insulation gap repaired. All parameters within spec. No adjustments required to charge or airflow.
compliance_notes: Refrigerant pressures checked at service ports only - system not opened. Lockout on both units for any physical access. Installation verified per local mechanical code requirements.

technician_notes: No cooling. Outdoor unit not running. Checked thermostat - calling for cool. Checked indoor air handler - blower running. Checked outdoor disconnect: fuses intact, voltage 240V at disconnect. Voltage at contactor line side: 240V. Voltage at load side: 0V. Contactor coil: 24V measured, pulling in confirmed audibly. Load-side terminals on contactor showing 0V - contacts welded open. Unusual failure mode. Replaced contactor. After replacement, unit started. Measured startup amps: 72A (LRA spec 76A), holding amps 12.8A (RLA 14A). Ran 20 minutes, checked pressures: suction 74 PSI, discharge 238 PSI at 96F ambient. Normal.
anomalies_flagged: Contactor contacts welded open - unusual failure, possible prior voltage spike
resolution: Failed contactor with welded-open contacts replaced. Unit started and verified cooling normally.
compliance_notes: Lockout applied to outdoor disconnect before contactor replacement. Confirmed no voltage at load terminals before work. PPE including insulated gloves during electrical work.

technician_notes: Quarterly inspection per service contract. Unit in heating mode, ambient 22F. Gas heat only - compressor locked out below 30F per controls. Checked heat section: burners firing all three stages cleanly. Measured CO at flue 14 ppm, acceptable. Supply temp 125F in full heat. Economizer: verified dampers closed and sealed, checked weatherstripping - intact. Roof around unit: minor ponding noted 2 feet from unit drain. Not currently causing issues but flagged for building maintenance. Electrical: all connections tight, checked with torque screwdriver. Filter change on 90-day cycle - changed out per schedule.
anomalies_flagged: Roof ponding near unit drain noted - flagged for building maintenance review
resolution: Routine inspection completed, filter changed per schedule. Unit operating normally in heating mode. Roof ponding documented for building management.
compliance_notes: Rooftop work: appropriate fall protection awareness, no edge exposure. Lockout on unit disconnect for filter change. Gas system not accessed.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Summer PM at 92F ambient. Replaced 20x20x1 filter. Outdoor coil cleaned - moderate dust and grass clippings on inlet, rinsed. Refrigerant: suction 78 PSI, discharge 248 PSI at 92F, subcooling 11F, superheat 10F - all within spec. Capacitor: 45+5 uF, measured 43.8/4.6 uF - acceptable but toward lower end. Documented for replacement consideration next PM. Contactor contacts slightly pitted but within tolerance. Indoor blower: checked belt-drive blower - belt tension good, no fraying. Checked motor bearings - no unusual noise. Drain pan clean.
anomalies_flagged: Dual-run capacitor reading toward lower tolerance limit - monitor
resolution: Filter replaced, coil cleaned. System within spec. Capacitor noted for potential replacement at next PM in 6 months.
compliance_notes: Standard lockout on outdoor and indoor disconnects. Refrigerant pressures verified with calibrated gauges - gauges calibrated within past 90 days.

technician_notes: Furnace not starting, thermostat calling for heat. Arrived, checked fault codes: 33 - limit circuit fault. Inducer running, no ignition attempt. Checked pressure switch: hose from inducer to switch - found crack in rubber hose at inducer port, leaking. Pressure switch not seeing adequate negative pressure, locking out inducer safety circuit before ignition can proceed. Replaced rubber pressure switch hose. Cleared fault, unit started normally. Ran through 3 heat cycles, limit did not trip. Checked actual limit switch continuity - normal.
anomalies_flagged: Cracked pressure switch hose at inducer port - caused fault code 33
resolution: Cracked pressure switch hose replaced. Fault cleared. Unit cycling normally on 3 consecutive heat calls.
compliance_notes: Gas supply on during diagnosis but only low-voltage circuit work performed. Full lockout applied before hose replacement due to proximity to gas train. Standard PPE.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: Spring PM. Checked inverter log: no faults. Outdoor coil clean - unit has coil guard, minimal debris. Checked refrigerant: suction 62 PSI, discharge 192 PSI at 68F ambient cooling mode. Superheat 8F, subcooling 9F. Normal for variable speed part-load. Replaced filter. Checked indoor coil - coil clean, no visible issues. Drain: clear, pan clean. Checked low-voltage wiring connections at air handler board - found one spade connector on Y2 wire slightly loose, pushed in and seated. Confirmed signal reaching outdoor unit. Checked communicating system setup codes - all correct.
anomalies_flagged: Loose Y2 spade connector at air handler control board - reseated
resolution: Loose connector reseated. Filter replaced. System operating normally.
compliance_notes: Lockout at both disconnects before accessing air handler electrical compartment. Low-voltage work only at control board.

technician_notes: Inspection requested after new homeowner purchase. Unit is 7 years old. Overall unit in fair condition. Refrigerant: suction 71 PSI, discharge 232 PSI at 94F ambient. Subcooling 8F, superheat 14F - slightly elevated superheat but within acceptable range. Contactor: contacts pitted, showed burn marks on one edge - approaching replacement. Capacitor: 45+5 uF, reading 38.2/4.1 uF - below tolerance, flagged. Compressor amp draw: 14.1A RLA, nameplate 14A - right at limit, elevated for ambient. Heat strip verified off. Coil clean, drain clear. Reported findings to homeowner with recommendation to replace capacitor and contactor.
anomalies_flagged: Dual-run capacitor below tolerance - 38.2/4.1 uF vs 45+5 rated
Contactor contacts pitted and burned - approaching end of service life
Compressor amp draw at nameplate limit
resolution: Inspection completed. Capacitor and contactor replacement recommended. Homeowner declined service today, will schedule separately.
compliance_notes: Lockout applied for all physical inspections. Refrigerant system not opened. Documented findings for homeowner records.

technician_notes: Building reporting loud banging noise from rooftop unit, not cooling. Arrived to find condenser fan blade had separated at hub and struck condenser coil. Fan motor shaft sheared. One blade had torn through three coil rows - significant coil damage. Unit shut down immediately. Assessed damage: fan motor destroyed, one blade, and coil damage to top section of condenser coil. Coil repair/replacement and fan motor/blade replacement needed. Parts ordered - ETA 3 days. Placed temporary lock on unit disconnect and tagged out. Building HVAC going to backup portable units.
anomalies_flagged: Condenser fan blade separated at hub - catastrophic failure
Condenser coil damaged by blade impact - 3 rows bent and punctured
Fan motor shaft sheared
resolution: Unit shut down and locked out. Damage assessed. Parts ordered. Temporary cooling arranged for building. Repair scheduled for parts arrival.
compliance_notes: Unit locked and tagged out with company lock - do not energize. Damage documentation photos taken. OSHA incident review not required as no personnel injury occurred. Parts to be sourced from authorized Carrier distributor.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Inspection at new commercial tenant space - unit was dormant for 6 months. Started unit, ran diagnostics. Initial startup: compressor ran but high pressure fault tripped at 15 seconds. Found outdoor fan running in reverse - capacitor connected to wrong terminals on fan motor after prior service by another contractor. Corrected wiring, restarted. Fan running correctly, high pressure dropped from 480 to 265 PSI, unit stabilized. Checked refrigerant after stabilization: suction 74 PSI, discharge 248 PSI at 95F ambient. Subcooling 10F. Coil was fairly clean given dormancy. Filter loaded - replaced.
anomalies_flagged: Condenser fan wired in reverse - prior service error, corrected
resolution: Fan motor wiring corrected. High pressure fault cleared. System operating normally. Filter replaced.
compliance_notes: Lockout on outdoor disconnect before correcting wiring. Verified correct rotation before closing cabinet. No refrigerant vented during fault condition.

technician_notes: Fall PM on SL280V. Checked heat exchanger with scope - clean and intact. Ignitor resistance 47 ohms, within 40-90 ohm range. Flame sensor reading 3.8 uA, good. Gas manifold pressure 3.5 in WC. CO at flue 10 ppm. Replaced filter - media filter was 8 months old, significantly loaded. Checked blower motor: direct drive ECM motor, checked speeds via test mode - all speeds operational. Measured supply temp 138F on high fire. Checked all low-voltage wiring, no loose connections. Verified thermostat calibration: set to 70, room reading 70F.
resolution: PM completed. Filter replaced. All combustion and safety parameters within spec. Unit ready for heating season.
compliance_notes: Gas system not accessed during this PM. Lockout for filter and blower compartment access. Standard PPE throughout.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: Short cycling complaint - unit running in 2 minute cycles. Thermostat showing cool but minimal cooling. Checked refrigerant: suction 28 PSI, discharge 60 PSI - extremely low, near atmospheric. System has lost most charge. Electronic leak detector survey: strong positive hit at outdoor coil header connection - brazed fitting. Visual confirmation of oily residue at fitting. Recovered remaining refrigerant (0.6 lbs R-410A). Brazed fitting repaired with silver braze after cleaning. Pressure tested with nitrogen to 300 PSI, held for 30 minutes. Evacuated to 300 microns, held for 15 minutes. Recharged with 4.2 lbs R-410A per nameplate. Suction 118 PSI, discharge 310 PSI at 80F ambient.
anomalies_flagged: Brazed fitting leak at outdoor coil header - oily residue confirmed
System nearly empty - lost approximately 3.6 lbs R-410A
resolution: Leak repaired by brazing. Pressure tested and verified. System evacuated and recharged. Cooling confirmed normal.
compliance_notes: R-410A recovered before opening system per EPA 608. Recovery cylinder used. Refrigerant log updated with recovered amount and recharge amount. Brazing: nitrogen purge maintained during brazing to prevent oxide formation inside copper lines. Fire extinguisher staged. PPE: safety glasses, gloves, face shield during brazing.

technician_notes: Spring PM. Outdoor unit: coil clean - good shape for 4-year-old unit. Capacitor dual-run 45+5 uF, reading 44.9/5.0 uF - excellent. Contactor contacts clean. Refrigerant check at 80F ambient: suction 74 PSI, discharge 228 PSI. Subcooling 10F, superheat 11F. Perfect. Indoor: replaced 20x25x1 filter. Coil clean. Drain pan and drain clear. Blower wheel clean. Checked thermostat programming - schedule programmed correctly. Checked all electrical connections, no issues. Told customer unit is in great shape for its age.
resolution: Filter replaced. System in excellent condition. No issues identified.
compliance_notes: Lockout at both disconnects for filter and coil inspection access. Standard PPE.

technician_notes: Winter PM. Ambient 18F. Heat-only operation confirmed correct per controls setup. Checked all three gas stages: stage 1 firing 250 MBH, stage 2 adding 250 MBH, stage 3 at 500 MBH total. CO at flue 11 ppm. Measured supply temp 142F on full heat. Filters changed per schedule. Checked economizer - dampers sealed, actuator disconnected for winter per building operator request. Electrical compartment: all connections tight. Checked heat exchanger surfaces through burner ports - no visible damage. Noted condensate drain heater inoperative - drain freeze risk in current temps.
anomalies_flagged: Condensate drain heater inoperative - freeze risk in current cold weather
resolution: Filters replaced. Drain heater failure identified. Part ordered for next visit.
compliance_notes: Rooftop access in winter conditions - appropriate footwear (non-slip) and cold weather PPE. Lockout for filter and electrical access. Gas system not accessed this visit.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Ice on refrigerant lines complaint, no cooling. Arrived: suction line fully iced from outdoor unit to air handler. Indoor unit blower not running. Checked blower - found ECM motor fault light on. Tested motor module - no response to control board. Checked 24V to module: 0V. Traced: fuse on control board blown. Found burnt wire near fuse holder - insulation melted, shorted to chassis. Repaired wire with proper gauge and connector, replaced fuse. Blower started. Allowed ice to melt before running compressor. After melt, started system, verified normal operation. Suction 70 PSI, discharge 225 PSI, superheat 12F.
anomalies_flagged: ECM blower motor fuse blown due to shorted wire at control board
Shorted wire with melted insulation - potential fire hazard
resolution: Damaged wire repaired, fuse replaced. Blower motor operational. Ice allowed to clear. System verified normal after restart.
compliance_notes: Lockout on indoor air handler before any electrical work. Shorted wire documented - evidence of prior overheating. Wire repair completed per NEC standards with proper connectors and wire gauge. No open wiring left in unit.

technician_notes: Pre-season inspection. Checked all safeties first. Limit switch tested: opened at 200F, reset at 155F, within spec. Pressure switch tested with manometer: tripped at -0.38 in WC, within range for this model. Rollout switch: normal position, reset manually to verify function. Ignitor tested: 120V confirmed, glow confirmed in 17 seconds. Flame sensor: 4.2 uA reading after 30-second warmup. Gas valve: staged correctly on thermostat call. Heat exchanger scope inspection: all cells clean, no cracks. CO at flue: 9 ppm. Filter was recently changed by homeowner - within 30 days.
resolution: All safeties and combustion components within specification. Unit ready for heating season.
compliance_notes: Gas system accessed only for pressure measurements at test ports. Lockout applied for limit switch test procedure. Combustion analysis completed per ACCA standards.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: Summer PM at 82F ambient. Inverter log check: one fault, E7 communication error 6 weeks ago, cleared itself. Likely interference event. No current fault. Refrigerant in cooling: suction 122 PSI, discharge 305 PSI, subcooling 10F, superheat 8F. Excellent. Outdoor coil: minimal debris. Indoor filter replaced. Air handler coil: clean. Drain: clear, treated with pan tabs. Checked all control wiring connections - all tight. Tested emergency heat function - resistance strips came on, 5kW strip confirmed drawing 20.8A at 240V. Returned to heat pump mode.
anomalies_flagged: Historic E7 communication fault - self-cleared, monitoring recommended
resolution: Filter replaced, drain treated. All systems normal. Historic fault noted for monitoring.
compliance_notes: Emergency heat test: 240V circuit loaded to 20.8A - appropriate PPE including insulated tools. Lockout re-applied after test. Standard procedures for refrigerant pressure check.

technician_notes: Squealing noise from indoor unit complaint. Arrived, confirmed loud bearing squeal from blower motor. Shut down system. Attempted to rotate blower wheel manually - very stiff, bearing failure confirmed. Motor is PSC type, direct drive. Checked model and serial, motor is 5 years old. Removed motor, confirmed seized bearing - heat discoloration on shaft. Replaced motor with identical HP and RPM rating. Reset blower speed taps to prior setting (medium-high). Restarted, blower running quietly. Amp draw 5.8A on medium-high, within spec.
anomalies_flagged: Blower motor bearing failure - seized
resolution: Failed blower motor replaced with matching unit. System restarted and confirmed quiet operation.
compliance_notes: Lockout on indoor air handler disconnect before motor removal. Motor weighs 12 lbs - proper lift technique used. Capacitor discharged before handling motor and capacitor connections.

technician_notes: Inspection following complaint of uneven temperatures in building - west zone too warm. Checked zone dampers: zone 1 (west) damper stuck at 30% open, actuator not responding to signal. Checked 24V signal at actuator terminals - signal present. Tested actuator manually - motor runs but drive gear stripped. Replaced actuator. Damper moved to 100% on test signal. Verified zone 1 supply temp match with setpoint. Also noted during inspection: outdoor coil screen partially blocked by bird netting installation by building owner, removed netting from coil face.
anomalies_flagged: Zone 1 VAV damper actuator gear stripped - damper stuck at 30%
Bird netting blocking condenser coil face - removed
resolution: Damper actuator replaced. Zone 1 operation verified. Bird netting removed from coil.
compliance_notes: Lockout on unit before accessing ductwork and damper locations. 24V control circuit only for actuator replacement.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Late summer PM. Filter replaced. Outdoor coil clean - minimal debris. Refrigerant at 88F ambient: suction 80 PSI, discharge 258 PSI, subcooling 10F, superheat 11F. Good. Capacitor: 45+5 uF, reading 44.6/4.9 uF. Contactor: contacts good. Blower amp draw: 7.2A on high. Indoor coil inspection: noticed slight discoloration at right side of evaporator coil face - possible early mold. Applied antimicrobial coil cleaner, rinsed. Drain clear. Advised homeowner to consider UV light installation.
anomalies_flagged: Early discoloration on evaporator coil face - possible mold, treated
resolution: Coil cleaned with antimicrobial treatment. Filter replaced. System operating normally. UV light recommended to homeowner.
compliance_notes: Antimicrobial coil cleaner applied per SDS instructions, not EPA-registered as pesticide for this application. Appropriate gloves and eye protection worn. Unit locked out before coil access.

technician_notes: Furnace not heating - thermostat calling but no response at all. Checked 24V transformer output: 27V - normal. Checked thermostat circuit: opened thermostat wiring, found R and C wires connected but W1 wire broken at connector inside unit. Wire had been pinched by service panel edge at previous service. Repaired wire with proper butt connector and replaced connector. Checked remaining wires - all intact. Cleared path for wire bundle, added protective sleeve at panel edge. Unit started normally on next thermostat call.
anomalies_flagged: W1 thermostat wire broken at service panel edge - pinched by prior service
Wire bundle needed protective sleeve at panel edge
resolution: Broken W1 wire repaired and spliced. Protective sleeve added. Unit responding to thermostat normally.
compliance_notes: Low-voltage work only. Lockout on furnace power switch before wire access. Wire repair using insulated connectors per NEC low-voltage requirements.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: High humidity complaint - system cooling but RH 68% indoors. System running continuously. Checked thermostat: dehumidification mode was disabled after power outage reset all settings to factory default. Re-enabled dehumidification in thermostat menu, set RH target to 50%. Also checked: refrigerant subcooling 9F (normal), superheat 8F (normal), indicating charge is correct. Checked indoor coil drain - clear. Ran system in enhanced dehumidification mode for 30 minutes, RH dropped from 68% to 59%.
anomalies_flagged: Dehumidification settings lost due to power outage - thermostat factory reset
resolution: Dehumidification settings restored in communicating thermostat. System back in humidity control mode.
compliance_notes: No HVAC system physical access required. Thermostat programming only. No lockout required.

technician_notes: Fall PM - end of cooling season. Replaced filter. Ran cooling mode check: suction 69 PSI, discharge 218 PSI at 82F ambient. Subcooling 9F. Superheat 13F. Normal. Capacitor: 45+5 uF, reading 44.1/4.8 uF. Contactor contacts clean. Covered outdoor unit per homeowner request for winter - advised homeowner to remove cover before starting in spring. Also checked for any critters - found mouse nesting material near contactor, cleaned out and treated area with deterrent spray (non-chemical, scent-based).
anomalies_flagged: Rodent nesting material found near contactor - cleaned, no wire damage
resolution: Nesting material removed, area treated. Filter replaced. System winterized per homeowner request.
compliance_notes: Lockout before cleaning nesting material from electrical area. Verified no wire insulation damage from rodent chewing. Unit cover applied by homeowner after lockout released.

technician_notes: Building reporting no heat in zone 3. Unit fault: E7 - ignition failure, 3 lockouts. Retrieved fault history: 8 ignition failures in past week. Checked ignitor: resistance 15 ohms - very low, cracked. Replaced ignitor. Tried to fire: inducer spins, ignitor glows, gas valve opens, no ignition. Checked gas at burner: lighter test - gas flowing. Checked spark ignitor polarity (different from glow - confirmed this model uses glow not spark). Realized flame sensor wire had been disconnected - hanging loose. Connected properly. Unit fired on first attempt, all three stages verified.
anomalies_flagged: Ignitor cracked - resistance out of spec (15 ohms)
Flame sensor wire disconnected - prior service error
resolution: Ignitor replaced. Flame sensor wire reconnected. Unit verified through 3 heat cycles.
compliance_notes: Gas system: lockout at unit gas valve before ignitor replacement. Confirmed gas off with manometer drop test. Reconnected flame sensor wire per wiring diagram.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: New home buyer inspection, unit is 6 years old. Found unit in reasonable condition. Refrigerant: suction 74 PSI, discharge 240 PSI at 90F ambient. Subcooling 9F, superheat 14F. Slightly elevated superheat but within range. Capacitor: 45+7.5 uF dual run. Measured 42.1/6.8 uF - toward low end, acceptable for now. Contactor: contacts showing moderate pitting, would recommend replacement within next year. Indoor coil: slight discoloration at edges suggesting prior moisture issue. Blower wheel: heavy debris on all blades - significantly reducing airflow. Cleaned blower wheel. After cleaning: static pressure dropped from 0.95 to 0.71 in WC.
anomalies_flagged: Blower wheel heavily loaded with debris - cleaned on site
Contactor contacts moderately pitted - replacement within 12 months recommended
Evaporator coil edge discoloration - possible prior moisture/mold
resolution: Blower wheel cleaned on site. Contactor and possible coil cleaning flagged for follow-up. Homeowner received written summary of findings.
compliance_notes: Lockout on both units for blower wheel cleaning. Refrigerant checked at ports - no system access.

technician_notes: Fall PM. Replaced media filter - was 9 months old, very loaded. Checked static with new filter: 0.48 in WC, normal. Heat exchanger scope: all cells clean. Ignitor: 52 ohms, good. Flame sensor: 3.6 uA, good. Gas manifold: 3.4 in WC. CO at flue: 11 ppm. Blower ECM: ran all modes, functioning. One item noted: flue pipe at roof penetration had missing storm collar, open gap around pipe. Not directly affecting unit but creates path for water and pests. Notified homeowner.
anomalies_flagged: Missing storm collar at flue pipe roof penetration - pest and water entry risk
resolution: PM completed normally. Storm collar absence documented and communicated to homeowner - recommend scheduling sheet metal repair.
compliance_notes: Gas system: pressure checked at test port only. No system access required. Lockout for filter and blower compartment. Storm collar repair is building envelope work - referred to appropriate contractor.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: High electric bill complaint and unit running constantly. Ambient 52F - shoulder season, heat pump should be running minimally. Found outdoor unit running at 100% capacity continuously. Checked communicating thermostat: outdoor temperature sensor reading -18F when actual ambient is 52F. Sensor failure causing unit to operate in emergency heat override mode. Traced outdoor temp sensor wire to outdoor unit, found sensor wire broken at connector entering control board, likely from rodent chewing. Replaced sensor and sealed entry point with caulk. After repair, unit dropped to stage 2 capacity, supply temp dropped from 130F to 102F, correct for ambient.
anomalies_flagged: Outdoor temperature sensor wire broken - rodent damage
Unit running at 100% due to false cold sensor reading
resolution: Outdoor temp sensor and wiring replaced. Entry point sealed. Unit returned to normal variable capacity operation.
parts_replaced: Outdoor ambient temperature sensor with wire
compliance_notes: Lockout on outdoor unit before accessing control board and sensor wiring. Low-voltage circuit only. Sealed entry point with appropriate non-flammable caulk.

technician_notes: Midsummer PM at 98F. Replaced filter. Condenser coil cleaning: heavy accumulation of airborne dirt, cleaned thoroughly with coil cleaner and low-pressure rinse. Post-cleaning refrigerant check: suction 76 PSI, discharge 236 PSI. Before cleaning, suction had been 68 PSI (slightly low) and discharge 252 PSI - high head pressure from coil restriction. After cleaning, numbers normalized. Capacitor 45+5 uF, reading 43.8/4.7 uF. Contactor clean. Confirmed drain clear. Delta T across evaporator coil: 18F split, indicating good latent and sensible cooling.
anomalies_flagged: Condenser coil heavily fouled - causing elevated head pressure before cleaning
resolution: Condenser coil cleaned. Refrigerant pressures normalized. System operating efficiently post-cleaning.
compliance_notes: Coil cleaner applied per SDS - non-acid based, pH neutral. Eye protection and gloves worn. Rinse water drained to ground (no chemical disposal concern with this cleaner type). Lockout applied before coil cleaning.

technician_notes: Insurance inspection for commercial building. Focused on heat section safety. Checked all burner safeties: limit, rollout, pressure switch. All tested within spec. Heat exchanger: scope inspection through burner ports - noticed one cell with visible rust staining inside secondary exchanger. Not a crack, appears to be condensate residue. Photographed. CO at flue: 16 ppm (within 25 ppm acceptable but higher than typical). CO at supply register: 0 ppm, no evidence of spillage. Documented for building owner. Recommend follow-up scope inspection at next PM in 6 months to track any progression.
anomalies_flagged: Rust staining in one secondary heat exchanger cell - not a crack, monitoring recommended
Flue CO at 16 ppm - within limit but trending high, monitoring recommended
resolution: Inspection completed. Heat exchanger integrity maintained but monitoring flagged. Documentation provided to building owner.
compliance_notes: Gas system: operational testing only, no physical access to gas train. Combustion analysis per ACCA and ASHRAE 62.2 residential standards applied as proxy for commercial setting. CO documentation retained in service file.

equipment_type: Trane XR15 Split System Heat Pump
technician_notes: Unit not cooling, outdoor unit running but very hot to touch on discharge line. Suction 52 PSI (low), discharge 340 PSI (very high), subcooling 22F (elevated), superheat 4F (very low). Classic overcharged system. Customer confirmed they had added refrigerant themselves using an at-home kit. System overcharged by approximately 1.8 lbs. Recovered 1.8 lbs R-410A, brought charge back to spec: suction 72 PSI, discharge 228 PSI, subcooling 10F, superheat 12F. Advised homeowner not to add refrigerant themselves and explained why.
anomalies_flagged: System overcharged by 1.8 lbs R-410A - homeowner added refrigerant with consumer kit
resolution: Excess refrigerant recovered. Charge corrected to spec. Homeowner educated on refrigerant handling regulations.
compliance_notes: R-410A recovered using certified recovery equipment per EPA 608. Recovered refrigerant logged. Consumer refrigerant addition is technically a violation of EPA 608 for R-410A without certification - documented for homeowner awareness but no regulatory reporting obligation from service tech side.

technician_notes: No heat, thermostat showing call. Arrived: inducer not starting. Checked 24V circuit at inducer relay: relay pulling in. Checked 120V at inducer motor: 120V present at connector. Motor not running. Tried to rotate inducer wheel manually: seized. Inducer motor bearing failure. Motor is 9 years old on a 15-20 year expected life. Replaced inducer motor assembly. After replacement, inducer started, pressure switch pulled, ignition sequence initiated. Unit fired, verified 3 heat cycles. Noted blower wheel had slight imbalance vibration - minor, documented.
anomalies_flagged: Inducer motor bearing failure - seized
Blower wheel slight imbalance vibration - minor, documented
resolution: Inducer motor assembly replaced. Unit firing and heating normally.
compliance_notes: Lockout on furnace 120V switch before motor replacement. Gas supply remained on but burner could not fire with inducer out. Verified gas off at unit valve during motor swap as additional precaution. All wiring reconnected per wiring diagram.

equipment_type: York YZV Variable Capacity Heat Pump
technician_notes: Inspection following renter complaint of intermittent cooling. Arrived: unit operating normally, no active fault. Checked inverter fault log: two E4 high pressure faults in past 3 weeks, both self-cleared. No current high pressure issue. Outdoor ambient 85F, discharge 302 PSI, within range for VS unit. Checked outdoor coil - found lower section obstructed by overgrown shrub, approximately 12 inches into coil face. Pruned shrub back. Checked wiring: one conduit connector at outdoor unit was loose, tightened. Refrigerant: suction 118 PSI, discharge 298 PSI post-prune, slightly lower.
anomalies_flagged: Shrub obstructing outdoor coil - pruned back
Loose conduit connector at outdoor unit
Two prior E4 high pressure faults likely related to coil obstruction
resolution: Shrub trimmed away from coil. Conduit connector secured. Discharge pressure reduced after airflow restored.
compliance_notes: Pruning done by tech - appropriate for minor landscape obstruction. Lockout on unit before conduit connector tightening. Refrigerant system not accessed.

technician_notes: Fall PM - transitioning out of cooling season. Ran cooling check: suction 70 PSI, discharge 215 PSI at 78F ambient. Subcooling 9F, superheat 12F. Good. Replaced filter. Outdoor coil: clean, rinsed debris from hurricane season. Capacitor: 45+5 uF, reading 44.5/4.9 uF - excellent. Contactor: clean contacts. Drain treated with pan tabs for offseason. Checked disconnect block: pull-out fuse holder had slight corrosion on fuse clips from coastal humidity. Cleaned with electrical contact cleaner and reinstalled. Voltage drop across fuses post-cleaning: less than 0.1V each.
anomalies_flagged: Disconnect fuse clips corroded from coastal humidity - cleaned
resolution: Fuse clips cleaned. Filter replaced. System winterized for reduced use season. All parameters normal.
compliance_notes: Electrical contact cleaner: non-conductive, fast-evaporating type used per SDS. Lockout applied before cleaning fuse clips. Verified voltage readings after cleaning with certified meter.
